title: rhynchogyna fallax
side_effects: Rhynchogyna fallax can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, skin irritation, and allergic reactions. Ingesting the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while topical application may lead to redness, itching, and swelling. In severe cases, improper use of Rhynchogyna fallax can trigger anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using any plant-based remedy to avoid such adverse reactions.
